rulings.Case Title: JAMES BARAGAR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, August 26, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Caterpillar Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant has satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present any admissible evidence creating a triable fact regarding asbestos content. The objections to Mr. Cohen¿s declaration are sustained. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES BARAGAR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, August 26, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Gibbs & Cox, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and plaintiffs¿ motion to compel are continued to August 31, 2010 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 220 on the court¿s own motion. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES BARAGAR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, August 26, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Air & Liquid Systems Corp., successor by merger to Buffalo Pumps¿ motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present any admissible evidence creating a triable fact whether Mr. Baragar was exposed to any asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. The objections to the declarations of Mr. Fening and Mr. Kenneth Cohen are sustained. =(220/HEK)

On calendar for Thursday, August 26, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Moore Bros.¿ motion for summary judgment is denied.
